text_fr: 72/115. Programme d'assistance des Nations Unies aux fins de l'enseignement, de l'ÃĐtude, de la diffusion et d'une comprÃĐhension plus large du droit international
text_en: 72/115. United Nations Programme of Assistance in the Teaching, Study, Dissemination and Wider Appreciation of International Law

text_fr: L'AssemblÃĐe gÃĐnÃĐrale ,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Rappelant sa  rÃĐsolution  2099 (XX)  du  20 dÃĐcembre  1965  portant  crÃĐation  du Programme d'assistance des Nations Unies aux fins de l'enseignement, de l'ÃĐtude, de  la  diffusion  et  d'une  comprÃĐhension  plus  large  du  droit  international,  qui  doit concourir  Ã   faire  mieux  connaÃŪtre  le  droit  international  en  tant  que  moyen  de renforcer  la  paix  et  la  sÃĐcuritÃĐ  internationales  et  de  promouvoir  les  relations amicales et la coopÃĐration entre les Ãtats,
text_en: Recalling its resolution 2099 (XX) of 20 December 1965 establishing the United Nations Programme of Assistance in the Teaching, Study, Dissemination and Wider Appreciation of International Law, which should contribute to raising awareness of international law as a means of strengthening international peace and security and promoting friendly relations and cooperation among States,

text_fr: RÃĐaffirmant que  le  Programme  d'assistance  est  une  activitÃĐ  de  base  de l'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies  et  qu'il  sous-tend,  depuis  plus  d'un  demi-siÃĻcle, les activitÃĐs  que  mÃĻne  l'Organisation  pour  mieux  faire  connaÃŪtre  le  droit international,
text_en: Reaffirming that the Programme of Assistance is a core activity of the United Nations and that it has underpinned, for more than half a century, the activities of the Organization to raise awareness of international law,

text_fr: ConsidÃĐrant que le Programme  d'assistance  contribue grandement  Ã  l'enseignement  et  Ã   la  diffusion  du  droit  international  auprÃĻs  des  juristes  de diffÃĐrents  pays,  systÃĻmes  juridiques  et  rÃĐgions  du  monde  depuis  plus  d'un demi-siÃĻcle et qu'il importe d'en assurer la continuitÃĐ pour les gÃĐnÃĐrations prÃĐsentes et futures de juristes,
text_en: Recognizing that the Programme of Assistance has made an important contribution to the teaching and dissemination of international law to lawyers from different countries, legal systems and regions of the world for more than half a century and that it is important to ensure its continuity for present and future generations of lawyers,

text_fr: Soulignant que le Programme d'assistance et en particulier les cours rÃĐgionaux de droit international des Nations Unies et de la MÃĐdiathÃĻque de droit international des Nations Unies concourent grandement Ã  promouvoir les activitÃĐs et programmes des Nations Unies relatifs Ã  l'ÃĐtat de droit,
text_en: Emphasizing that the Programme of Assistance and in particular the United Nations Regional Courses in International Law and the United Nations Audiovisual Library of International Law play an important role in promoting United Nations rule of law activities and programmes,

text_fr: RÃĐaffirmant que l'accroissement de la demande d'activitÃĐs de formation et de diffusion  dans  le  domaine  du  droit  international  impose  de  nouvelles  tÃĒches  au Programme d'assistance,
text_en: Reaffirming that the increased demand for training and dissemination activities in the field of international law imposes new tasks on the Programme of Assistance,

text_fr: Consciente qu'il importe que le Programme d'assistance atteigne effectivement  ceux  Ã   qui  il  s'adresse  et  que  la  question  des  langues  soit  prise  en compte, tout en sachant que les ressources sont limitÃĐes,
text_en: Mindful of the importance of the effective reach of the Programme of Assistance and of the inclusion of the issue of languages, while recognizing that resources are limited,

text_en: Noting with appreciation that resources have been included in the programme budget for the annual organization of the United Nations regional courses in international law and the enrichment of the United Nations Audiovisual Library of International Law,

text_en: Recognizing that international law should be prominent in the teaching of the legal disciplines of all universities,

text_en: Convinced of the need to encourage States, international and regional organizations, universities and institutions to further support the Programme of Assistance and to intensify their activities aimed at promoting the teaching, study, dissemination and wider understanding of international law, including those of particular interest to persons from developing countries,

text_fr: RÃĐaffirmant qu'il serait souhaitable, pour exÃĐcuter le Programme d'assistance, d'utiliser dans toute la mesure possible les ressources et les moyens fournis par les Ãtats  Membres,  les  organisations  internationales  et  rÃĐgionales,  les  universitÃĐs,  les institutions et d'autres partenaires,

text_en: Expressing once again the hope that the selection of highly qualified lecturers for the seminars to be held in the framework of the fellowship programmes in international law will take into account the need to ensure representation of the major legal systems and a balance between the different regions,

Salue les efforts dÃĐployÃĐs par la Division de la codification pour mettre Ã  jour les publications juridiques de l'Organisation des Nations Unies, grÃĒce auxquels le dÃĐlai de parution des publications a ÃĐtÃĐ considÃĐrablement rÃĐduit et des supports de formation  en  droit  ont  pu  ÃŠtre  mis  au  point,  et  engage  la  Division  Ã   continuer  de chercher les moyens de poursuivre ces efforts pendant le prochain exercice biennal, si les ressources disponibles le permettent ;

items_en: 2. Authorizes the Secretary-General to implement in 2018 and 2019 the activities set out in his report 1 , including those listed below, to be funded from the regular budget:
(a) The International Law Fellowship Programme for at least 20 fellows;
(b) The United Nations Regional Courses in International Law for Africa, Asia and the Pacific and Latin America and the Caribbean, each for at least 20 fellows;
(c) Maintenance and enrichment of the United Nations Audiovisual Library of International Law;
(d) Dissemination of the legal publications and conferences of the Audiovisual Library in developing countries, subject to the availability of adequate resources;

3. Also authorizes the Secretary-General to extend the activities referred to in paragraph 2 above, to be financed from voluntary contributions received in accordance with paragraphs 15, 24 and 25 of the present resolution;

4. Commends the Codification Division of the Office of Legal Affairs of the Secretariat for the cost-saving measures introduced by the General Assembly in the International Law Fellowship Programme and the United Nations Regional Courses in International Law to increase the number of fellowships funded from the regular budget, taking into account the number of candidates;

5. Authorizes the Secretary-General to award additional fellowships funded from within the programme budget for the Programme of Assistance and from voluntary contributions received in accordance with paragraph 25 of the present resolution;

6. Requests the Secretary-General to consider allowing candidates present in the host country, bearing the full cost of their participation, or from countries willing to do so, to participate in training programmes;
7. Authorizes the Secretary General to award at least one scholarship in 2018 and one in 2019 for the Hamilton Shirley Amerasinghe Memorial Fellowship on the Law of the Sea;

8. Requests the Secretary-General to include in the regular budget, for its consideration by the General Assembly, resources for the Hamilton Shirley Amerasinghe Memorial Fellowship on the Law of the Sea for the biennium 2018-2019 in the event that voluntary contributions are not sufficient to award at least one fellowship per year;

9. Also requests the Secretary-General to continue to include in the proposed programme budget for the biennium 2020-2021 resources for the International Law Fellowship Programme, the United Nations Regional Courses in International Law for Africa, Asia and the Pacific and Latin America and the Caribbean each year, and for the maintenance and enrichment of the United Nations Audiovisual Library of International Law;

10. Expresses its appreciation to the Secretary-General for his activities under the Programme of Assistance, in particular his efforts in 2017 to strengthen, expand and improve training and dissemination activities in the field of international law within the framework of the Programme of Assistance;

11. Notes with appreciation the continuing active work of the Secretary-General in enriching the United Nations Audiovisual Library of International Law, which contributes greatly to the teaching and dissemination of international law throughout the world;

12. Recognizes the importance of the United Nations legal publications prepared by the Office of Legal Affairs, and reiterates its request to the Secretary-General to issue, in various forms, the printed version of which is essential for developing countries, as referred to in his previous report; 3

13. Welcomes the efforts of the Codification Division to update the legal publications of the United Nations, through which the time for issuance of publications has been significantly reduced and legal training materials have been developed, and encourages the Division to continue to explore ways to continue these efforts in the next biennium, if resources permit;
14. Takes note with appreciation of the publication of the ninth edition of the International Law Commission and its work and of the United Nations Juridical Yearbook 2013;

15. Welcomes the publication of the English version of the Manual of International Law , which is a valuable resource for the teaching of a wide range of fundamental subjects of international law, which will enable academic institutions in developing countries to promote the teaching of international law in developing countries, and requests Member States to make the necessary voluntary contributions so that such training material can be published in French and, if funds permit, in the other official languages of the United Nations and disseminated in those countries;

16. Requests the Codification Division to continue to update and expand the websites listed in the annex to the report of the Secretary-General, whose usefulness for the dissemination of international law texts and advanced legal research is invaluable;
17. Requests the use of interns and research assistants to prepare documentation for the United Nations Audiovisual Library of International Law and the Handbook of International Law;
18. Welcomes the efforts of the Codification Division to revitalize and organize the United Nations regional courses in international law, which constitute an important training activity;

19. Expresses its appreciation to Ethiopia, Chile and Thailand for hosting in 2017 the United Nations Regional Courses in International Law for Africa, Latin America and the Caribbean and Asia and the Pacific;
20. Expresses its appreciation to the African Union for its continued valuable contribution to the United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Africa;

21. Calls once again upon the Codification Division to cooperate with the African Institute of International Law, an institution of higher education and research in international law for the development of Africa, in the implementation of the activities of the Programme of Assistance;

26. Remercie les  Ãtats  Membres  qui  ont  versÃĐ  des  contributions  volontaires au Programme d'assistance ;

27. Prie le  SecrÃĐtaire  gÃĐnÃĐral  de  lui  rendre  compte,  Ã   sa  soixante-treiziÃĻme session, de l'exÃĐcution du Programme d'assistance en 2018 et de lui prÃĐsenter, aprÃĻs avoir  consultÃĐ  le  ComitÃĐ  consultatif  pour  le  Programme  d'assistance,  des recommandations sur le Programme dans les annÃĐes Ã  venir ;

28. DÃĐcide d'inscrire  Ã   l'ordre  du  jour  provisoire  de  sa  soixante-treiziÃĻme session  la  question  intitulÃĐe  ÂŦ Programme  d'assistance  des  Nations  Unies  aux  fins de l'enseignement, de l'ÃĐtude, de la diffusion et d'une comprÃĐhension plus large du droit international Âŧ.
items_en: 22. Expresses its appreciation to the Hague Academy of International Law for the valuable contribution it continues to make to the Programme of Assistance, which has enabled beneficiaries of the International Law Fellowship Programme to participate in the Programme while attending the courses of the Academy;

23. Notes with appreciation the contribution of the Academy to the teaching, study, dissemination and wider understanding of international law, and calls upon Member States and interested organizations to welcome its call for continued and, where possible, increased financial contributions to enable it to carry out its activities, including summer courses, regional courses and programmes of the Centre for Study and Research in International Law and International Relations;

24. Requests the Secretary-General to continue to publicize the Programme of Assistance and to invite Member States, universities, philanthropic foundations, other relevant national and international institutions and organizations, and individuals, on a regular basis, to make voluntary contributions to finance the Programme or to assist in its implementation or expansion through other means;

25. Reiterates its request to Member States and interested organizations, institutions and individuals to make voluntary contributions to fund the United Nations Audiovisual Library of International Law and to enable the Codification Division to organize the United Nations Regional Courses in International Law, which are a useful complement to the International Law Fellowship Programme;
26. Expresses its appreciation to those Member States that have made voluntary contributions to the Programme of Assistance;

27. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its seventy-third session on the implementation of the Programme of Assistance in 2018 and, after consultation with the Advisory Committee on the Programme of Assistance, to submit recommendations on the Programme of Assistance in the coming years;

28. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its seventy-third session the item entitled "United Nations Programme of Assistance in the Teaching, Study, Dissemination and Wider Appreciation of International Law".

text_en: 67th plenary meeting 7 December 2017
